title: Don't Be a Grinch; Keep Holding
author: Dylan Lewis, Jason Moser
description: The stock market experienced a post-holiday hangover, with the S&P 500 down about 2% since Christmas. This was likely due to some end-of-year profit-taking and tax loss harvesting. However, the market has had a strong year overall, with the S&P 500 up around 26-27% year-to-date. Analysts are also keeping an eye on consumer credit card debt and delinquencies, which could impact discretionary spending in 2025.

description: President Trump's aggressive trade policies, including reciprocal tariffs, have already driven the S&P 500 into correction territory. title: Trump May Be 'Smoking In The Dynamite Shed' As The Big, Beautiful Bill Drives Bond Yields Higher

title: S&P 500 Holds Near Records as Strong GDP Cools Hopes for Early Fed Cuts
author: Itai Smidt
description: The U.S. economy grew 4.3% in Q3, beating expectations and pushing Treasury yields higher, which dampens prospects for early Fed rate cuts in 2026. While strong consumer spending supports earnings, the market is rotating away from mega-cap tech toward financials, defense, and materials. The S&P 500 holds near records with modest gains, while the Nasdaq pauses after recent strength. Metals surge on structural demand, and GLP-1 leader Novo Nordisk rallies on FDA approval of its obesity pill.

title: Google Rolling Out Gmail Address Change Feature: Here Is How It Works
author: Mohd Haider
description: Google has begun rolling out a new feature allowing users to change their @gmail.com addresses, initially launching in India. The feature lets users replace their existing Gmail address with a new one while keeping the old address as an alternate, with all account data preserved. Users can log in with either address, though the new address cannot be reused for another account for 12 months. Analysts remain bullish on Alphabet, with Citigroup maintaining a Buy rating and JPMorgan reaffirming Overweight.

title: A Year of Market Fear — and Resilience: Breaking Down the VIX in 2025
author: Mike Zaccardi, Cfa, Cmt
description: 2025 was marked by significant market volatility despite ending with resilience. The VIX spiked dramatically in April (above 60) following trade war rhetoric and policy uncertainty, but the S&P 500 recovered strongly with a 35%+ rally from May onward. Despite macro noise around tariffs, inflation, and Fed policy, the market delivered 15-20% returns with a maximum drawdown of only 19%, rewarding investors who focused on price action over headlines.

title: Stock Market Investors Got a Warning From Fed Chair Jerome Powell in 2025. History Says This Will Happen in 2026.
author: Trevor Jennewine
description: The S&P 500 has surged 18% in 2025 despite tariff-related economic uncertainty, but Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and other policymakers warn that stocks are trading at historically expensive valuations. With a CAPE ratio of 39.4—a level last seen during the dot-com bubble—historical patterns suggest the market could decline 4% by December 2026 and 20% by December 2027. However, strong AI-driven earnings growth and Wall Street's median target of 8,011 for the S&P 500 in December 2026 offer some optimism.

title: Global Credit Cards Transaction Value to Exceed $17.73T by 2030 and Digital-Only Virtual Cards to Record 6.91% CAGR, Reports Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global credit cards market is projected to grow from USD 14.83 trillion in 2025 to USD 17.73 trillion by 2030, with a CAGR of 3.64%. Growth is moderated by the rise of digital wallets and Buy Now, Pay Later solutions, particularly among younger consumers. North America dominates the market while Asia-Pacific experiences the fastest expansion. Credit card issuers are intensifying competition through premium rewards programs and integrating wallet capabilities to maintain relevance.

title: 3 Reasons to Buy Ethereum Before January 2026
author: Emma Newbery
description: Ethereum is positioned for growth driven by three factors: expansion in stablecoin and real-world asset tokenization markets (with over $180 billion in tokenized assets on Ethereum), potential regulatory clarity from pending crypto legislation, and institutional adoption exemplified by JPMorgan Chase's tokenized money market fund launch on Ethereum. Despite being down 12% over the past year and trading 40% below its all-time high, analysts predict Ethereum could reach $11,800 by 2030, though investors should maintain caution given cryptocurrency volatility.
